name: Bridget
text: The Next button should be centered, not left-aligned.

name: Bridget
text: The Back button is too small and misplaced; it should be bottom-aligned, consistent with the other screens.

name: Bridget
text: Suggestion: gray out previously-selected sections as the user progresses, showing a 'ladder' of past choices with the current step highlighted. (A concern was raised that this could re-introduce confusion about what to click; the reviewer felt it wouldn't.)

name: Bridget
text: The Discovery icon is oddly placed; it should be centered.

name: Bridget
text: Circular UI elements look clickable but aren't — likely a source of tester confusion.

name: Bridget
text: The easy/medium/advanced color coding isn't applied to the Discovery section yet; it needs updating.

name: Bridget
text: Scenario answer options don't accurately reflect real senior-level roles. The reviewer (a senior director with 40+ meetings/week) felt the scenarios were off.

name: Bridget
text: The 'correct' answer is consistently the longest, most-worded option — this needs fixing so the answer isn't guessable by length.

name: Bridget
text: The word problems are too long; there's a risk of users disengaging. A simpler quiz format was requested for the opening flow.

name: Bridget
text: Competency category labels (e.g. 'AI Evaluation', 'Data Literacy') need tooltip definitions.

name: Bridget
text: Periodic self-reassessment: prompt users to re-evaluate their competencies at set intervals.

name: Bridget
text: Performance: the app currently uses Claude Opus (slower but higher quality); Sonnet is faster but lower quality. Explore a mixed-model approach for different generation tasks, and pre-generating content before the user enters the flow.

name: Bridget
text: Suggestion: prompt Claude to optimize data access, processing, and parsing for speed gains.

name: Bridget
text: The games (Family Feud, Wheel of Fortune) were well-received. Idea: a shareable team link for group play at team gatherings, with team points.

name: Bridget
text: The new UI was broadly praised — testers Storm, Andrea, Caria, and Emily were enthusiastic.
